name: Carbonnade (Belgian Beer Stew)
recipeIngredient: 2 Tbsp Red Wine Vinegar, or red wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef into 1 x 2 inch strips. Remove the ham from the bone and cut it into cubes.
2. Brown the beef and ham in oil in a large skillet. Lift the meat out, sprinkle with 1 tsp salt, and set aside.
3. Brown the onions in the same oil. Lift and set aside.
4. Drain all but 3 tbsp of the oil and reserve for sauteing walnuts later.
5. Sift the flour into the hot oil to make a light brown roux. Gradually add 1 1/2 cups of beef broth, stirring until the mixture boils.
6. Add the remaining broth, salt, pepper, sugar, herbs, and garlic. Layer the meat, onions, and carrots alternately in a large casserole dish.
7. Add the sauce and enough beer to cover the meat. Cover and cook in a 300°F oven for 2 1/2 hours (or cook on the stove top). Check occasionally and add more beer if needed.
8. Shortly before serving, saute the walnuts in the reserved oil until they are crisp. Do not scorch them. Add to the stew.
9. Just before serving, stir in the vinegar and Scotch whiskey.
